DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF MARIN THOMPSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS' REGENTS CAB CO'S AND SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI'S RESPONSES TO PLTF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (CCP 2031.310; 2031.320) IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 25, 2011. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO FURTHER MEET AND CONFER IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY MADE AFTER THIS MOTION WAS FILED. PLAINTIFF IS TO FILE AND SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL STATEMENT BY APRIL 15, 2011 STATING WHAT, IF ANYTHING, REMAINS IN DISPUTE AND WHAT FURTHER PRODUCTION AND EFFORTS BY DEFENDANTS TO LOCATE DOCUMENTS ARE REQUIRED. DEFENDANTS ARE TO FILE AND SERVE A REPLY BY APRIL 20, 2011 STATING WHY THEY BELIEVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, AND THE SCOPE OF THE SEARCH TO LOCATE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS, ARE SUFFICIENT. PARTIES ARE TO NOTIFY THE COURT IF THE MOTION SHOULD BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Defts' Regents Cab Co'S And San Francisco Independent Taxi'S Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Docs, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Ccp 2031.310; 2031.320)
Off Calendar Apr-04-2011 Continued To Apr-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF MARIN THOMPSON'S Motion To Compel Defendants' Regents Cab Co'S And San Francisco Independent Taxi'S Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Docs, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Ccp 2031.310; 2031.320) is continued to April 25, 2011. The parties are ordered to further meet and confer in light of defendant's supplemental responses to discovery made after this motion was filed. Plaintiff is to file and serve a supplemental statement by April 15, 2011 stating what, if anything, remains in dispute and what further production and efforts by defendants to locate documents are required. Defendants are to file and serve a reply by April 20, 2011 stating why they believe supplemental responses, and the scope of the search to locate documents that are the subject of plaintiff's requests, are sufficient. Parties are to notify the court if the motion should be taken off calendar. Judge: Marla Miller, Not reported =301/MM
